raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The Enhanced Consumer Protections for Preventive Care bill (AB 2258) passed the California State Assembly on a bipartisan basis.",
    "AB 2258 would require health plans and health insurance to cover services integral to recommended preventive services without out-of-pocket costs.",
    "A recent study found that as little as $10 in cost sharing for HIV prevention medication (PrEP) doubled the rate at which patients abandon their prescriptions.",
    "Federal guidance under the Affordable Care Act requires health insurers and health plans to cover both recommended preventive care and integral services without out-of-pocket costs.",
    "AB 2258 will strengthen existing law to ensure access to preventive health care services regardless of a federal court ruling on the Affordable Care Act.",
    "PrEP, whether in pill or injection form, is considered the most effective tool in ending the HIV epidemic.",
    "Addressing and eliminating barriers to HIV screenings and prevention services such as PrEP is critical for reducing new HIV infections."
  ]
}
```
